data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Faces User Backlash Over Claude AI Performance Decline
data.description: Anthropic is facing significant dissatisfaction from users due to a decline in the performance of its Claude AI chatbot, according to Fortune magazine. Several developers and heavy users have reported that the Claude AI model has recently shown a noticeable decrease in performance, including difficulties in following instructions, opting for inappropriate shortcuts in certain situations, and making more errors in complex workflows.

data.title: Kevin Warsh Reveals Bitcoin Startup Stake Ahead of Fed Chair Hearing
data.description: Kevin Warsh has disclosed an equity stake in the Bitcoin payments startup Flashnet, according to ethics filings submitted prior to his Senate confirmation hearing for the position of Federal Reserve chair. According to NS3.AI, the hearing is anticipated to commence as early as this week, though it is more likely to begin next week. The filings also revealed other assets potentially exceeding $100 million, with Warsh committing to divest several holdings that could present conflicts of interest.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Faces Challenges in Strait of Hormuz Shipping Efforts
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is encountering significant challenges as he attempts to ensure the safe passage of commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg posted on X that despite his efforts, Trump remains largely isolated in this endeavor. The strategic waterway, a critical route for global oil shipments, has been a focal point of geopolitical tensions, with various nations expressing concerns over security and stability in the region. Trump's administration has been working to rally international support to safeguard the strait, but the response has been tepid. The situation underscores the complexities of navigating international diplomacy and security in one of the world's most vital maritime corridors.

data.title: Figure and Hastra Expand Tokenized Credit Platform with Auto Loans
data.description:
Figure and Hastra are broadening their tokenized credit platform by incorporating auto loans, thereby extending the range of real-world assets available to decentralized finance (DeFi) investors beyond home equity products. According to Cointelegraph, Democratized Prime, a decentralized lending marketplace on Figure Markets, is introducing auto finance as its inaugural new asset class. This initiative is part of a broader strategy to create a marketplace where various types of consumer credit can be issued, traded, and funded onchain, as announced on Tuesday.

Michael Tannenbaum, CEO of Figure, emphasized the strategic nature of this development, noting that the platform has already originated over $22 billion in onchain loans. This move represents an early test of whether tokenized private credit can extend beyond home-equity products into mainstream consumer lending. Such a shift could potentially broaden DeFi’s access to real-world yield while also introducing the credit risks associated with subprime-style loan markets. Figure launched Hastra in 2025, with its public debut and rollout occurring later that year. Initially launched on Solana (SOL), the platform was designed as an extension of Figure’s lending ecosystem, utilizing its loan origination and credit infrastructure to bring real-world assets (RWAs) onchain.

Simultaneously, Hastra is expanding to Ethereum-compatible (EVM) chains, thereby opening access to a larger DeFi ecosystem and extending its existing credit system, including home equity loan exposure, to new chains. A Figure spokesperson informed Cointelegraph that Hastra will begin with Ethereum (ETH) as part of its expansion into EVM chains. They also confirmed that the auto finance product will initially launch on Solana before being rolled out on Ethereum around June. Despite these advancements, bringing consumer loans onchain does not eliminate the inherent risks associated with these assets. Non-prime auto loans can have higher default rates, particularly in weaker economic conditions. Additionally, there are concerns regarding regulation, transparency, and the performance of these blockchain-based credit products under stress or during volatile market conditions.

Earlier this month, Bernstein analysts expressed a bullish outlook on Figure, suggesting that the blockchain-based lender might be undervalued. They assigned an “Outperform” rating and a $67 price target, nearly double its recent trading price. This optimistic perspective follows growth in Figure's tokenized lending business, with loan originations exceeding $1.2 billion in March and first-quarter volumes reaching $2.9 billion. Figure went public on September 11, 2025, listing on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol FIGR.

data.title: USDC Treasury Mints 250 Million USDC
data.description: According to NS3.AI, Whale Alert has reported the minting of 250 million USDC at the USDC Treasury. This development is significant as it reflects ongoing activities in the stablecoin market, which can influence liquidity and trading dynamics. The minting of such a large amount of USDC could have implications for market participants, potentially affecting supply and demand within the cryptocurrency ecosystem.

data.title: Aave Switches Swap Routing to ParaSwap Following CowSwap Frontend Attack
data.description: Aave has transitioned its interface swap routing to ParaSwap in response to a recent frontend attack on CowSwap. According to NS3.AI, Aave clarified that the security of its interface and underlying protocol remained unaffected by the incident. As a precautionary measure, CowSwap temporarily disabled swap endpoints for integrators.

data.title: Aave DAO Approves $25 Million Deal with Aave Labs
data.description: Aave DAO has finalized an agreement to allocate $25 million in stablecoins and 75,000 AAVE tokens to Aave Labs. According to NS3.AI, in exchange, Aave Labs will direct all revenue generated from Aave-branded products to the DAO treasury. This decision resolves a prolonged disagreement concerning revenue from a swap feature on the Aave website. The dispute had led three long-standing service providers to announce their departure, citing concerns over increasing centralization of power around Aave Labs.

data.title: Carlyle Group Raises $1.5 Billion for New Asset-Backed Investment Fund
data.description:
Carlyle Group has successfully raised $1.5 billion in the first round of funding for its newly established asset-backed investment fund, according to BlockBeats. This move is part of the private equity giant's ongoing expansion of its credit business. 

Sources familiar with the matter revealed that pension funds, sovereign wealth funds, and other institutional investors participated in the Carlyle Asset-Backed Income Fund. Unlike most credit funds with fixed terms, this fund will adopt a 'perpetual' structure, meaning it will not have a set expiration date. Due to the sensitivity of the information, the sources requested anonymity.

data.title: Plastics Prices Surge Amid US-Israeli Conflict with Iran
data.description: Dow and Exxon are among the companies increasing prices for plastics as the industry faces supply disruptions due to the ongoing US-Israeli conflict with Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of geopolitical tensions on the sector. The conflict has led to significant supply chain challenges, prompting companies to adjust pricing strategies to cope with the disruptions. The situation underscores the broader economic implications of geopolitical conflicts on global industries.

data.title: Fitch Ratings: Middle East Energy Shock Reflects in Higher Inflation and Bond Yields
data.description: Fitch Ratings has reported that the energy shock originating from the Middle East is now manifesting in increased inflation and bond yields. According to Jin10, this development is impacting global financial markets as the region's energy dynamics continue to influence economic indicators. The rise in inflation and bond yields is attributed to the ongoing geopolitical tensions and supply chain disruptions in the Middle East, which have led to fluctuations in energy prices. Fitch Ratings highlights the need for investors to remain vigilant as these economic shifts could have broader implications for global markets.

data.title: Grayscale Predicts $2.2 Trillion Crypto Demand from Generational Wealth Transfer
data.description: Grayscale has projected that a generational wealth transfer could lead to approximately $2.2 trillion in new demand for cryptocurrencies. According to NS3.AI, this estimate is based on the assumption that 2% of the assets transferred by older generations will be invested in the crypto market. Currently, Americans over the age of 60 possess around $110 trillion in assets. In contrast, 45% of Millennials and Gen Z are reported to own cryptocurrencies, compared to only 18% of older generations.

data.title: US officials: The United States has not imposed a complete blockade on the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: A U.S. official stated that the U.S. Navy is not currently escorting any ships through the Strait of Hormuz, but has conveyed to merchant vessels (those not entering or leaving Iranian ports) that the waterway is safe and encouraged to pass. The official indicated that the U.S. has not imposed a complete blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, but only a blockade on vessels traveling along the entire Iranian coastline to and from Iranian ports. The official also stated that the U.S. dispatched a destroyer through the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend to demonstrate the existence of a safe passageway. (Jinshi)

data.title: BlackRock's Crypto ETFs Attract $935 Million in Q1 2026
data.description: BlackRock's crypto exchange-traded products (ETPs) attracted $935 million in net inflows during the first quarter of 2026, as reported in the firm's Q1 earnings. According to NS3.AI, these products have accumulated $32 billion over the past year. BlackRock also reported a total of $130 billion in net inflows across all its products for the quarter. Larry Fink, BlackRock's CEO, noted that this quarter marked one of the strongest starts to the year in the company's history. Data from Dune indicates that BlackRock clients hold approximately 890,000 of the 1.6 million Bitcoin in ETFs, surpassing Fidelity's 12% market share.

data.title: Pakistani President: Pakistan will strive to ensure the sustainability of the US-Iran peace process.
data.description: Pakistani President Asif Ali Zardari stated that Pakistan should continue to actively engage with the United States, Iran, and other major regional and global countries to ensure the sustainability of the US-Iran peace process. During a meeting with Prime Minister Sheikh Shahbaz, Zardari said that Pakistan demonstrates its position as a responsible nation in the international community by providing a platform for such crucial dialogues. (Xinhua)

data.title: Aave: The CowSwap frontend attack has not yet affected the security of Aave's API and underlying protocol.
data.description: Aave stated on the X platform that it is aware of the attack on the CowSwap frontend, but the incident did not affect the security of the Aave interface or underlying protocol. As a precaution, the CowSwap team has temporarily shut down the exchange endpoint for integrators, and transactions within the same Aave interface have been switched to ParaSwap routing where available to ensure transaction continuity for users.

data.title: WTI crude oil futures plunged 7% on the day, currently trading at $92.11 per barrel.
data.description: According to Gate data, WTI crude oil fell 6% intraday, currently trading at $92.17 per barrel. WTI crude oil futures plunged 7% intraday, currently trading at $92.11 per barrel. Spot silver rose 5% intraday, currently trading at $79.36 per ounce.

data.title: ECB's Makhlouf Emphasizes Commitment to Inflation Target
data.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) is unwavering in its commitment to achieving its inflation target, according to ECB Governing Council member Gabriel Makhlouf. According to Jin10, Makhlouf reiterated the central bank's focus on maintaining price stability as a primary objective. He emphasized that the ECB is dedicated to taking necessary measures to ensure inflation aligns with its goals. Makhlouf's comments come amid ongoing discussions about monetary policy adjustments in response to economic conditions. The ECB continues to monitor inflation trends closely, aiming to maintain economic stability across the eurozone.

data.title: The Central Bank of Russia plans to mandate real-name registration for cryptocurrency transactions and comprehensively strengthen the supervision of fund flows.
data.description: The Central Bank of Russia announced plans to require all cryptocurrency traders to complete Know Your Customer (KYC) verification and restrict unregistered users from withdrawing cryptocurrency assets from domestic platforms, aiming to strengthen regulation of the domestic cryptocurrency market. According to the proposed rules, Russia will require domestic exchanges to fully implement real-name registration and prohibit users from transferring funds from domestic custodial wallets to overseas non-custodial wallets. Simultaneously, regulators plan to require residents to declare their overseas cryptocurrency holdings to enhance monitoring of capital outflows. Central Bank Deputy Chairman Vladimir Chistyukhin stated that the new regulations will not prohibit individuals from holding or using cryptocurrency assets, but the focus is on improving transparency and compliance to prevent money laundering risks. This policy is expected to be implemented in July this year along with a new round of cryptocurrency regulatory legislation. Analysts believe this move reflects Russia's attempt to control capital flows by strengthening cryptocurrency regulation amidst economic pressure. (DL News)

data.title: FOMC Member and Chicago Fed President Goolsbee to Join Panel Discussion at Semafor 2026 World Economic Conference
data.description: FOMC member and Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee is set to participate in a panel discussion at the Semafor 2026 World Economic Conference in ten minutes. According to Jin10, this event is expected to provide insights into the current economic landscape and monetary policy considerations. Goolsbee's participation comes as the global financial community closely monitors central bank actions and economic indicators.

data.title: CLARITY Act Faces Critical Two-Week Senate Window Amid Industry Concerns
data.description: The CLARITY Act is facing a crucial two-week period for Senate action, with industry advocates cautioning that a failure this spring could postpone U.S. crypto legislation until 2030. According to NS3.AI, the odds of the act passing this year have dropped to 58% from 82% in February, as indicated by Polymarket. The Senate Banking Committee has yet to schedule a markup, while banks and crypto firms remain divided over whether stablecoin issuers should pass yields to users.
